Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). There is also low total CO₂, indicating possible metabolic acidosis. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. Microalbuminuria is present. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney disease; urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended.
